headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash
summary: reached general availability at I/O.

headline: Antigravity 2.0
summary: launched as a standalone multi-agent development suite — desktop app, CLI, SDK, Managed Agents in the Gemini API, and an enterprise tier (Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform).

headline: Gemini Spark
summary: — a persistent AI agent inside the Gemini app that runs continuously on dedicated Google Cloud VMs, completing long-running tasks and integrating with connected apps without requiring the user's device to remain open.

headline: Anthropic Project Glasswing — partners cleared to share findings.
summary: In a May policy update, Anthropic allowed its Project Glasswing partners (AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorganChase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Palo Alto Networks) to share Claude Mythos-based threat intelligence with each other.

headline: OpenAI GPT-5.5 Instant
summary: became the default ChatGPT model on May 5, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant.

headline: OpenAI Deployment Company ("DeployCo")
summary: launched May 11 as a majority-owned subsidiary targeting enterprise AI deployment at scale.

headline: ByteDance Lance
summary: — Trending #1 on HuggingFace today (trending score 439; 446 likes).

headline: Unsloth Qwen3.6 GGUF ports
summary: — Two Unsloth quantizations of Alibaba's Qwen3.6 family are trending heavily today.

headline: Supertone supertonic-3
summary: — Multilingual text-to-speech model (trending score 324; 492 likes) covering 25+ languages including EN, KO, JA, AR.

headline: MiniCPM-V-4.6
summary: (OpenBMB) — Trending score 329 with 166K downloads and 824 likes.

headline: Gemma 4
summary: (Google, April 2) — Featured at Google I/O May 20 in the "What's new in Gemma open model family" session.

headline: DeepSeek V4-Pro / V4-Flash
summary: (April 24) — V4-Pro: **1.6T parameters (49B active MoE)**, 1M-token context, MIT license.

headline: Kimi K2.6
summary: (Moonshot AI, April 20) — **1T-parameter MoE (32B active)**, Modified MIT license, free weights on HuggingFace.

headline: Open-weights close the gap to 6 points.
summary: Artificial Analysis's May 2026 Intelligence Index shows the gap between the three leading open-weight models and the best closed frontiers has narrowed from 13 to 6 index points.

headline: No single frontier winner across benchmarks.
summary: Per aggregated leaderboard tracking for May 2026: DeepSeek V4-Pro wins SWE-bench and LiveCodeBench; GPT-5.4 wins MMLU and LMArena; Claude Opus 4.6/4.7 (thinking) wins GPQA and Aider.

headline: Kimi K2.6 leads SWE-Bench Pro (58.6) and HLE-Full (54.0).
summary: On SWE-Bench Pro — a professional software engineering task subset — K2.6 at 58.6 edges GPT-5.4 at 57.7 and Claude Opus 4.6 at 53.4.

headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash benchmark claims — independence unverified.
summary: Google's I/O benchmark slide: 76.2% Terminal-Bench 2.1 vs.

headline: EU AI Act Omnibus — high-risk deadlines delayed 16 months
summary: (May 7).

headline: Cohere acquires Aleph Alpha at $20B combined valuation
summary: (announced April 24).

headline: China blocks Meta's $2B acquisition of Manus
summary: (April 27).

headline: Nebius acquires Eigen AI for $643M
summary: (May 1).

headline: MLX vs. llama.cpp: the 40K-token ceiling quantified.
summary: A May 2026 Towards AI analysis measured Ollama 0.19's MLX backend (switched from llama.cpp on March 30) at up to **3× faster token generation** for standard workloads on Apple Silicon — but found the advantage largely disappears beyond ~40K tokens, where MLX's memory management patterns become less

headline: MiniCPM-V-4.6 tops HuggingFace on-device multimodal ranking.
summary: OpenBMB's lightweight multimodal vision-language model is the most-liked on-device model currently trending on HuggingFace (824 likes, 166K downloads, trending score 329).

headline: Community Qwen3.x chat-template fix for MLX/llama.cpp/vllm.
summary: `froggeric/Qwen-Fixed-Chat-Templates` (331 likes, trending score 153) provides corrected Jinja chat templates for Qwen3.x and Qwen3.6 model families across mlx, llama.cpp, vllm, LM Studio, and Ollama.
